page: Microsoft Azure D48alds_v6 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The system is configured with 48 vCPUs derived from 24 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The memory configuration consists of 96.0 GB of RAM, yielding a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The server includes 2835 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities, highlighted by a top-tier Geekbench multi-core score of 20503.0 and strong stress-ng multi-core performance. The AMD EPYC processor's instruction set, including AVX-512 and cryptography extensions, supports strong performance in Passmark CPU encryption and extended instructions. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is strong at 3648.29 GB/sec, though large block uncached read and write speeds fall into the average range.

The primary tradeoff of the D48alds_v6 is its low resource density regarding memory, providing only 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This makes it less suitable for memory-intensive applications or large-scale LLM text generation, as evidenced by weak performance in large-model LLM text generation. However, for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints, the high vCPU count and substantial local storage of 2835 GB offer a cost-efficient profile by avoiding the premium associated with memory-optimized instances.

This server is well-suited for compute-bound tasks such as software compilation, ray tracing, image processing, and database operations, supported by strong Passmark database and Redis SET benchmarks. It is also highly capable for small-model LLM inference, text processing, and multi-threaded decompression tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D48alds_v6 is an x86_64 compute-oriented server powered by a dedicated 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores. It features a low memory-to-vCPU ratio, offering 96.0 GB of RAM (2.0 GB per core), paired with 2835 GB of local storage. Operating on Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance delivers top-tier Geekbench multi-core performance and strong single-core capabilities. While memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks is average, its small-block cached read bandwidth is strong. The server's hardware profile presents a clear tradeoff: high compute density and substantial local storage versus limited memory capacity. This configuration is highly efficient for compute-heavy, memory-light workloads. It is best suited for software compilation, database operations, Redis caching, and small-model machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48alds_v6 is an x86_64 server with 48 vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and 2835 GB local storage. Powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC CPU, it delivers top-tier multi-core performance for compute-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48alds_v6 is a compute-oriented server featuring 48 vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and 2835 GB local storage powered by an AMD EPYC processor.
tagline: A high-compute x86_64 platform featuring forty-eight vCPUs and substantial local storage optimized for compute-intensive, low-memory workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor
96.0 GB RAM providing a compute-optimized 2.0 GB per core ratio
2835 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier multi-core performance in Geekbench and LLM text generation benchmarks
Best suited for software compilation, database operations, and small-model inference
